commit.message:
fix: replace fyne.io/systray with direct CGO NSStatusItem

fyne.io/systray called dispatch_main() which causes a SIGTRAP when Wails'
NSApp is already running on the main thread. Replace with a separate
tray_darwin.m that uses dispatch_async(dispatch_get_main_queue()) to create
an NSStatusItem without taking over the main run loop.

- Remove fyne.io/systray dependency entirely
- tray_darwin.m: NSStatusItem + NSMenu via dispatch_async (no dispatch_main)
- tray_darwin.go: Go bindings + //export callbacks for show/quit
- tray_stub.go: no-op for non-darwin builds
- app.go: startup calls initTray(a) directly (no goroutine needed)

commit.message:
feat: add MCP server and --json structured output for bash scripts (#11)

* feat: add MCP server and --json structured output for bash scripts

Add stdio MCP server (cm-repos) exposing 8 tools that wrap the existing
bash helper scripts for structured AI agent discovery. All 6 user-facing
scripts now support a --json flag producing {ok, tool, data, error}
JSON on stdout.

MCP server features:
- 8 tools: repo_status, validate_repo, validate_all, sync_deps,
  tag_repo, tag_all, project_add, project_status
- Input validation (regex guards for repo names, versions, URLs,
  Go module paths) and path traversal protection
- Per-tool timeouts (30s–10min) with structured error responses
- Auto-detection of CM_REPO_BASE (env var → walk up cwd → ~/repo)
- Windows Git Bash resolution
- Bootstrap launcher with first-run dependency install

Bash --json changes:
- All scripts parse --json before positional args
- All error paths emit valid JSON when --json is active
- validate-all.sh aggregates validate-repo.sh --json results
- Consistent {ok, tool, data, error} contract across all scripts

Also: bump version to 1.1.0, update READMEs, add RELEASES.md entry,
update marketplace and plugin metadata.

Closes #5

commit.message:
G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).

commit.message:
Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Heredoc denim quoting</issue_title>
> <issue_description>I don't currently handle heredoc delim quoting.
From the bash reference:
> 
> 
> The format of here-documents is:
> 
> [n]<<[−]word
>         here-document
> delimiter
> The shell does not perform parameter and variable expansion, command
substitution, arithmetic expansion, or filename expansion on word.
> 
> If any part of word is quoted, the delimiter is the result of quote
removal on word, and the lines in the here-document are not expanded. If
word is unquoted, delimiter is word itself, and the here-document text
is treated similarly to a double-quoted string: all lines of the
here-document are subjected to parameter expansion, command
substitution, and arithmetic expansion, the character sequence \newline
is treated literally, and ‘\’ must be used to quote the characters ‘\’,
‘$’, and ‘`’; however, double quote characters have no special meaning.
> 
> 
> Write some test cases in dparser that use a quoted heredoc. Single
quotes, double quotes, and backlashes , and combinations. Add some
simple examples to the command_acceptance module.
> 
> Then see if the contents is appropriately parsed. And try and improve
the parser where required.</issue_description>

commit.message:
Fix GitHub Pages 404s for reports and missing callgrind links in bench report (#207)

* Initial plan

* Fix GitHub Pages 404s and missing callgrind links in bench report

Three issues fixed in ci.yml:

1. Download ci-reports to docs/ (not docs/reports/) in deploy job.
   The artifact is uploaded with two paths (reports/ and bench_raw.json),
   so GitHub Actions preserves the reports/ prefix in the artifact.
   Downloading to docs/reports/ caused double-nesting at
   docs/reports/reports/... (404 for point_cloud_viewer.html and
   test-results.md). Downloading to docs/ maps reports/ correctly to
   docs/reports/.

2. Add family field to each callgrind matrix fixture so the
   family/id key format (e.g. direct-primitives/g4box-box-20x30x40-v1)
   matches what generate_bench_report.py uses when looking up callgrind
   links. Previously only the bare id was used as the key, causing
   callgrind_links.get(fixture_id) to always return None.

3. Update callgrind_links JSON key from bare id to family/id format.

* Rewrite city generation with continuous height function

- Eliminate all holes: use inverse-mapping pixel scan with fully continuous
  height evaluation instead of discrete column lookups with rounding
- Follow planet curvature: bottom edge uses surfaceR = sqrt(R²-t²) - R
  so cities wrap along the circular surface
- Smooth edge taper: remove Math.max(1,...) clamp so envelope naturally
  trails off to 0 with no step at the edges
- Towers at least 2px wide: defined as continuous rectangular regions
  (2-3px) in tangent space
- Interpolated mound noise for smooth continuous variation

* Refactor data to Uint32Array packed RGBA, redesign cities as multi-layer noise

- Data refactor: SceneObject.data is now Uint32Array with packed RGBA per pixel.
  Colors are baked into the data — no more passing color/cityColor params.
  rebuildImageData just copies data to ImageData buffer. packColor() helper added.
  All callers updated: planet.ts, meteor.ts, game.ts, city.ts.

- City redesign: Multi-layer value noise (3 octaves) for organic mountain-like
  terrain. Tower layer (noise threshold 0.55) and spire layer (threshold 0.7)
  add skyscraper peaks. Cities grow from planet rim following exact curvature.
  Fully continuous height function — no discrete columns, no holes at any angle.

- Removed CITY_DATA_VALUE constant. City pixels identified by packed color match.
  countCityPixels takes cityPacked param.

- Enriches active escrows with current ledger state
- Reconstructs completed/expired escrows from metadata alone

Changes:
- MetadataCache: Extended schema to include amount, deadline, finish_after
- Added migration logic to add new columns to existing databases
- BlockchainEscrowTracker.list_company_escrows(): Now queries metadata for history
- Added _reconstruct_historical_escrow() for completed/expired escrows
- app.py create_escrow: Now stores amount and deadline in metadata

Benefits:
✅ Completed escrows remain visible in history
✅ Merchant can verify all past transactions
✅ No data loss from ledger finalization
✅ Single source of truth still blockchain (with metadata enrichment)

Implement blockchain-based escrow tracking

Replace in-memory escrows_db with live XRP Ledger queries:

- Create BlockchainEscrowTracker class to query escrows from ledger
- Implement MetadataCache (SQLite) for storing order metadata and delivery key hashes
- Update all API endpoints to use blockchain queries instead of local storage
- Store only minimal metadata (order_id, delivery_key_hash) instead of full escrow details
- Derive escrow status from blockchain state (pending, claimable, completed, expired)
- Keep delivery key store separate for verification

Benefits:
- Single source of truth (blockchain)
- Survives app restarts
- No database consistency issues
- Real-time on-chain data

Modified endpoints:
- /api/escrow/create: Records metadata after creating escrow
- /api/merchant/escrows: Queries ledger for all company escrows
- /api/demo/escrows: Queries ledger instead of local storage

commit.message:
fix: normalize structured LLM content in serialization and memory updater (#1215)

* fix: normalize ToolMessage structured content in serialization

When models return ToolMessage content as a list of content blocks
(e.g. [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]), the UI previously displayed
the raw Python repr string instead of the extracted text.

Replace str(msg.content) with the existing _extract_text() helper in
both _serialize_message() and stream() to properly normalize
list-of-blocks content to plain text.

Fixes #1149

Also fixes the same root cause as #1188 (characters displayed one per
line when tool response content is returned as structured blocks).

Added 11 regression tests covering string, list-of-blocks, mixed,
empty, and fallback content types.

* fix(memory): extract text from structured LLM responses in memory updater

When LLMs return response content as list of content blocks
(e.g. [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]) instead of plain strings,
str() produces Python repr which breaks JSON parsing in the memory
updater. This caused memory updates to silently fail.

Changes:
- Add _extract_text() helper in updater.py for safe content normalization
- Use _extract_text() instead of str(response.content) in update_memory()
- Fix format_conversation_for_update() to handle plain strings in list content
- Fix subagent executor fallback path to extract text from list content
- Replace print() with structured logging (logger.info/warning/error)
- Add 13 regression tests covering _extract_text, format_conversation,
  and update_memory with structured LLM responses

* fix: address Copilot review - defensive text extraction + logger.exception

- client.py _extract_text: use block.get('text') + isinstance check (prevent KeyError/TypeError)
- prompt.py format_conversation_for_update: same defensive check for dict text blocks
- executor.py: type-safe text extraction in both code paths, fallback to placeholder instead of str(raw_content)
- updater.py: use logger.exception() instead of logger.error() for traceback preservation

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preserve chunked structured content without spurious newlines

* fix: restore backend unit test compatibility

commit.message:
Fix escrow claim timing: add finish_after field to API

Problem: Frontend was checking if 47 hours had passed (hardcoded 1 hour before deadline on 48-hour period), causing 'Too early' message even though escrow was actually claimable after just 5 seconds.

Solution:
- Add finish_after field to DeliveryEscrow model
- Backend now includes finish_after in API response
- Frontend checks actual finish_after time instead of hardcoded calculation
- Escrow can now be claimed immediately after 5-second finish_after window

Files changed:
- xrp_escrow/models.py: Add finish_after field to DeliveryEscrow
- xrp_escrow/client.py: Pass finish_after when creating DeliveryEscrow
- web-component/backend/app.py: Include finish_after in API response and stored data
- web-component/templates/merchant.html: Check against finish_after instead of hardcoded time

Result: Users can now validate and claim escrows immediately without waiting, as intended.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
